channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech

item.title: Utah bans fluoride in public drinking water, a first in the US
item.description: Utah has become the first state to ban fluoride in public drinking water, despite widespread opposition from dentists and national health organizations.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: An AI-based drone that speeds up the detection and monitoring of wildfires is presented in Germany
item.description: EBERSWALDE, Germany (AP) — A company that specializes in early wildfire detection has developed a new, AI-based drone it says will help speed up the detection, location and monitoring of fires.
item.dc:creator: PIETRO DE CRISTOFARO, Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: Nueve tendencias de CRM para 2025: la IA redefine la ecuación del cliente
item.description: La inteligencia artificial (IA) tiene el potencial de transformar todos los aspectos de una empresa, pero sobre todo la gestión de las relaciones con los clientes (CRM), las funciones comerciales de cara al exterior de ventas, marketing y servicio al cliente.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: G2 Names INE 2025 Cybersecurity Training Leader
item.description: INE, a global leader in networking and cybersecurity training and certifications, is proud to announce it is the recipient of twelve badges in G2’s Spring 2025 Report, including Grid Leader for Cybersecurity Professional Development, Online Course Providers, and Technical Skills Development, which h
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: Nvidia-backed CoreWeave slashes IPO size and price amid investor concerns
item.description: CoreWeave plans to reduce the size of its U.S. initial public offering and price its shares below the indicated range, a person familiar with the matter told Reuters on Thursday, dampening expectations that the listing would boost investor appetite for IPOs.
item.dc:creator: Reuters, Fast Company

item.title: What is 'classified' information? What are 'secure' communications? Here's a primer
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The Trump administration's use of a popular messaging app to discuss sensitive military plans — with a journalist on the text chain — is raising questions about security and the importance of safeguarding the nation's secrets.
item.dc:creator: DAVID KLEPPER, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: 3 keys to maximizing data strategies
item.description: Business leaders know it’s crucial to use identity-driven customer data to make smart decisions. But often, they get stuck because they don’t have a unified view of their customers and prospects. That’s a recipe for poor business outcomes.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: With the rise of genAI, it’s time to follow Apple’s Security Recommendations
item.description: Apple’s Safari browser has a really useful password management feature, which is now also available as a standalone app called Passwords.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: What’s an email mask? Here’s why tech experts say you should be using one
item.description: You’ve heard of burner phones. What about burner email? So much of the internet now requires that you hand over your email address before you’re able to use any services—from an app you’ve downloaded to signing up for a newsletter or redeeming a special offer online.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Fast Company

item.title: Cyber leaders seek to demonstrate ‘ROI’ in face of DOGE cuts
item.description: The DOGE impact on federal cyber spending remains to be seen, but cyber program leaders are hoping to avoid any major cuts by emphasizing the ROI. The post Cyber leaders seek to demonstrate ‘ROI’ in face of DOGE cuts first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: Justin Doubleday, Federal News Network

item.title: Los Angeles sheriff's department to retest 4,000 DNA samples after using faulty kits for 8 months
item.description: LOS ANGELES (AP) — The Los Angeles County Sheriff’s Department said this week it will retest 4,000 DNA samples and open an internal investigation after learning that it used potentially flawed test kits for eight months.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Kyndryl extends Google Cloud partnership for AI-based mainframe modernization
item.description: Kyndryl and Google Cloud are expanding their partnership to help customers use generative AI to move data off the mainframe and into the cloud.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Senate Republican: Taylor Swift could write song about 'terrible breakup' between Musk, liberals
item.description: Sen. Bernie Moreno (R-Ohio) joked Thursday about the “terrible breakup” between Elon Musk and liberals, as Democrats denounce the tech billionaire’s prominent role in the Trump administration. After Sen. Elizabeth Warren (D-Mass.
item.dc:creator: Julia Shapero, The Hill

item.title: Figure skating embraces the future with new technology, modern concepts to engage athletes and fans
item.description: BOSTON (AP) — Ashley Wagner and Ben Agosto know what it's like to step off the ice at the figure skating world championships and feel as though they've conquered the sport. And what it feels like when the notoriously fickle sport bites back.
item.dc:creator: DAVE SKRETTA, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Agencies prepare to be resilient through ‘multidimensional’ risks and threats
item.description: From cyber attacks to natural disasters, agency continuity planning has become more “multidimensional” than ever before. The post Agencies prepare to be resilient through ‘multidimensional’ risks and threats first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: Justin Doubleday, Federal News Network

item.title: Qualcomm launches global antitrust offensive against Arm, accusing it of stifling competition
item.description: Qualcomm has launched a global antitrust offensive against Arm Holdings, accusing its longtime partner of anti-competitive practices in regulatory complaints filed across three continents.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Microsoft abandons data center projects as OpenAI considers its own, hinting at a market shift
item.description: OpenAI is reportedly contemplating building its own data center as Microsoft backs off on its own infrastructure investments. OpenAI has privately discussed building and operating its first data center to house storage, which is essential for developing sophisticated AI models.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Judge allows newspaper copyright lawsuit against OpenAI to proceed
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — A federal judge has ruled that The New York Times and other newspapers can proceed with a copyright lawsuit against OpenAI and Microsoft seeking to end the practice of using their stories to train artificial intelligence chatbots. U.S.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Teams successful on 52.2% of ball/strike challenges during spring training robot umpire test
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — Teams won 52.2% of their ball/strike challenges during the spring training test of robot umpires, and catchers had a markedly better success rate than pitchers.
item.dc:creator: RONALD BLUM, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Trump family crypto firm launches stablecoin
item.description: World Liberty Financial, a cryptocurrency venture created by President Trump and his sons, announced plans Tuesday to launch a stablecoin. The stablecoin, USD1, will be pegged to the U.S. dollar. Stablecoins are cryptocurrencies tied to assets, like fiat currency, to maintain a more stable price.
item.dc:creator: Julia Shapero, The Hill

item.title: DOGE staffer allegedly ran company providing services to hacking group
item.description: US Department of Government Efficiency tech advisor Edward Coristine previously ran a small infrastructure provider that offered services to a cybercriminal group, it has been alleged.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Signalgate: There’s an IT lesson here
item.description: You know how IT admins are always warning employees about best practices for security? They’re always mandating which apps to use, which to avoid and which devices can safely connect to corporate networks.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Cyprus to fund desalination plants at hotels to reduce impact of water shortages on tourism
item.description: NICOSIA, Cyprus (AP) — Cyprus said Wednesday it plans to subsidize construction of private desalination plants at hotels to ensure the tourism-reliant island nation has enough fresh water too see it through busy summer seasons when the demands of millions of visitors put a severe strain on dwindling
item.dc:creator: MENELAOS HADJICOSTIS, Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: Databricks’ TAO method to allow LLM training with unlabeled data
item.description: Data lakehouse provider Databricks has unveiled a new large language model (LLM) training method, TAO that will allow enterprises to train models without labeling data.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Microsoft’s newest AI agents can detail how they reason
item.description: If you’re wondering how AI agents work, Microsoft’s new Copilot AI agents provide real-time answers on how data is being analyzed and sourced to reach results.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Gee whiz, Gboard! An invaluable new Android typing upgrade
item.description: Ahh — don’t you just love the feeling of a fresh ‘n’ zesty virtual upgrade? Here in the land o’ Android, that doesn’t mean only major new Android versions (and, alas, the woeful waiting that often accompanies ’em).
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Open-source Styrolite project aims to simplify container runtime security
item.description: Today Edera launched a new open-source project called Styrolite to bring tighter controls to the interactions between containers and Linux kernel namespaces, at a layer below where Open Container Initiative (OCI) runtimes like containerd operate.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: DoD shepherds CMMC through Trump deregulatory initiative
item.description: The Trump administration has a '10 out, one in' rule for new regulations, but DoD officials are confident they can finalize the CMMC requirements soon. The post DoD shepherds CMMC through Trump deregulatory initiative first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: Justin Doubleday, Federal News Network

item.title: Warning for developers, web admins: update Next.js to prevent exploit
item.description: Developers and web admins using the Next.js framework for building or managing interactive web applications should install a security update to plug a critical vulnerability.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Napster sold to tech commerce company for $207 million
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — A brand that was notoriously connected to music piracy before reemerging as a subscription music service has been sold to Infinite Reality for $207 million.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Protecting your daughter from deepfakes and online abuse
item.description: Artificial intelligence-generated images and videos that use someone’s likeness typically target women without their consent. The CyberGuy unpacks how to protect loved ones.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: DoorDash to offer eat first, pay later option to customers
item.description: DoorDash and Klarna are partnering to offer consumers a buy now, pay later option when ordering food delivery.
item.dc:creator: Alicia Nieves, NewsNation

item.title: Chinese robot's kung fu moves will make your jaw drop
item.description: A humanoid robot developed in China has transformed from a nimble dancer to performing kung fu moves with surprising precision and balance.
item.dc:creator: Fox News
